task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenClinica dashboard or logged-in interface is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Access the 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' study.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active study is shown as 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' (or 'DM-TRIAL-2024') in the OpenClinica interface.
A file upload dialog, CRF management screen, or success message showing that '/home/ga/vital_signs_crf.xls' has been uploaded to OpenClinica.

task_completion.description: Export a properly paginated and formatted printable version (HTML or PDF) of the blank Vital Signs CRF.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A print preview, PDF viewer, or export dialog displaying the blank Vital Signs CRF with proper form formatting (containing 'vital signs' and 'systolic'/'diastolic').

task_completion.description: Save the exported CRF to the Downtime_Forms directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager showing the exported HTML or PDF file being saved to or located in '/home/ga/Documents/Downtime_Forms'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenClinica GUI to upload and export the CRF.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenClinica web interface and browser print/save dialogs, rather than terminal commands bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: The exported file is a properly formatted HTML or PDF, not a screenshot or raw text dump.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved file has a .pdf or .html extension and appears as a structured form, not an image file (.png, .jpg) or plain text file (.txt).

integrity.description: The exported CRF contains genuine content from the uploaded template.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported document displays actual form fields and text (e.g., 'vital signs', 'systolic', 'diastolic') derived from the OpenClinica system, not manually typed dummy text in a word processor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target study is 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' (identifier: DM-TRIAL-2024). The CRF template to upload is located at '/home/ga/vital_signs_crf.xls'. The exported printable CRF (HTML or PDF) must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/Downtime_Forms' and must contain the text 'vital signs' and either 'systolic' or 'diastolic'.
